articles.title: One in a million: celebrating the customers shaping AI’s future
articles.summary: A recent celebration highlighted the innovative customers who are pushing the boundaries of AI technology, with over a million users contributing to its development. These pioneers are shaping the future of AI by leveraging its capabilities to drive business growth, improve efficiency, and solve complex problems. Their contributions are helping to accelerate AI adoption and create new opportunities for industries worldwide.

articles.title: Continuously hardening ChatGPT Atlas against prompt injection
articles.summary: OpenAI is continuously enhancing ChatGPT Atlas to protect against prompt injection attacks, which involve manipulating the model with malicious input. This hardening process aims to improve the model's security and reliability, ensuring it can withstand attempts to exploit its vulnerabilities. By doing so, OpenAI is strengthening the foundation of its AI technology to provide safer and more trustworthy interactions.

articles.title: Evaluating chain-of-thought monitorability
articles.summary: Researchers are exploring "chain-of-thought monitorability", a concept that evaluates the ability to track and understand the decision-making process of AI models. This innovation aims to improve transparency and explainability in AI, enabling developers to identify biases and errors. By monitoring the thought process of AI, this technology has the potential to increase trust and reliability in AI-driven systems.

articles.title: Deepening our collaboration with the U.S. Department of Energy
articles.summary: Microsoft has announced a strengthened partnership with the U.S. Department of Energy to advance innovation in fields like artificial intelligence, clean energy, and quantum computing. This collaboration aims to drive breakthroughs in emerging technologies and tackle complex challenges. By working together, Microsoft and the DOE will accelerate the development of cutting-edge solutions to benefit the environment, economy, and society.

articles.title: Updating our Model Spec with teen protections
articles.summary: Meta has updated its model specification to include new protections for teenagers, aiming to enhance online safety and security for young users. The updated model focuses on limiting sensitive content and interactions, promoting a healthier online environment. This move demonstrates a commitment to prioritizing teen well-being in the digital space.

articles.title: Gemini 3 Flash: frontier intelligence built for speed
articles.summary: Gemini 3 Flash is a cutting-edge intelligence platform designed for exceptional speed, enabling faster and more efficient processing of complex data. This innovative technology is poised to revolutionize the field of artificial intelligence, allowing for quicker insights and decision-making. By prioritizing speed, Gemini 3 Flash aims to unlock new possibilities in AI applications and beyond.

articles.title: Gemma Scope 2: helping the AI safety community deepen understanding of complex language model behavior
articles.summary: The Gemma Scope 2 tool has been introduced to aid the AI safety community in better understanding complex language model behavior. This innovation enables researchers to analyze and visualize the decision-making processes of large language models, providing valuable insights into their strengths and weaknesses. By deepening our understanding of these models, Gemma Scope 2 can help improve their safety and reliability.

articles.title: Improved Gemini audio models for powerful voice experiences
articles.summary: Google has upgraded its Gemini audio models, enabling more accurate and efficient voice recognition for a range of applications. This improvement is expected to enhance voice experiences, allowing for better speech-to-text capabilities and more seamless interactions with virtual assistants. The updated models will likely have a significant impact on the development of voice-powered technologies.

articles.title: Deepening our partnership with the UK AI Security Institute
articles.summary: A leading tech company has announced a partnership with the UK AI Security Institute to enhance AI security and innovation. This collaboration aims to drive advancements in AI-powered security solutions, protecting against emerging threats. By combining expertise, the partnership seeks to foster a more secure and trustworthy AI ecosystem.

articles.title: Strengthening our partnership with the UK government to support prosperity and security in the AI era
articles.summary: A major tech company has announced a strengthened partnership with the UK government to drive AI innovation and support national prosperity and security. This collaboration aims to harness AI's potential to boost economic growth, improve public services, and enhance cybersecurity. By working together, the UK government and the tech company hope to establish the country as a global leader in responsible AI development and deployment.

articles.title: Tokenization in Transformers v5: Simpler, Clearer, and More Modular
articles.summary: The latest Transformers v5 update introduces a revamped tokenization system, making it simpler, clearer, and more modular. This new system allows for easier integration of custom tokenizers and improved handling of special tokens, enabling more efficient and accurate natural language processing. By streamlining tokenization, the update paves the way for more advanced language models and applications.

articles.title: The Open Evaluation Standard: Benchmarking NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Nano with NeMo Evaluator
articles.summary: NVIDIA has introduced the Open Evaluation Standard, a benchmarking tool that assesses the performance of AI models, with the Nemotron 3 Nano being the first model evaluated using the NeMo Evaluator. This standard aims to provide a unified framework for comparing AI model performance, enabling more accurate and fair comparisons. The Open Evaluation Standard has the potential to accelerate AI innovation by establishing a common language for model evaluation.

articles.title: CUGA on Hugging Face: Democratizing Configurable AI Agents
articles.summary: CUGA, a platform for creating customizable AI agents, has been integrated into Hugging Face, a popular AI hub, to democratize access to configurable AI models. This collaboration enables developers to easily build, train, and deploy tailored AI agents, lowering the barrier to entry for AI innovation. By making CUGA available on Hugging Face, the AI community can now leverage a wider range of customizable AI capabilities.

articles.title: New in llama.cpp: Model Management
articles.summary: The latest update to llama.cpp introduces model management, allowing users to easily manage and switch between different AI models. This innovation enables more flexibility and customization, making it simpler to adapt the AI to specific tasks and applications. By streamlining model management, llama.cpp enhances its usability and versatility for developers and users alike.

articles.title: Codex is Open Sourcing AI models
articles.summary: Codex is open-sourcing its AI models, allowing developers to access and build upon the technology. This move is expected to accelerate innovation in the field of artificial intelligence and promote collaboration among researchers and developers. By making its models open-source, Codex is democratizing access to AI and paving the way for new applications and advancements.

articles.title: Our New Sam Audio Model Transforms Audio Editing
articles.summary: The new Sam audio model revolutionizes audio editing by allowing for precise and efficient editing of audio files, enabling users to make complex changes with ease. This innovative model uses AI to analyze and understand audio content, making it possible to edit audio with unprecedented accuracy and speed. By transforming the audio editing landscape, Sam has the potential to democratize high-quality audio production and open up new creative possibilities.

articles.title: Scaling LLMs to Larger Codebases
articles.summary: Researchers are exploring ways to scale Large Language Models (LLMs) to larger codebases, enabling them to better understand and generate code. This innovation has the potential to revolutionize software development by improving code completion, debugging, and optimization. By scaling LLMs, developers can tap into more efficient and automated coding processes, transforming the way they build and maintain complex software systems.

articles.title: Flock Exposed Its AI-Powered Cameras to the Internet. We Tracked Ourselves
articles.summary: Flock, a company providing AI-powered cameras for neighborhood watch programs, inadvertently exposed its cameras to the internet, allowing researchers to track and access footage. This security lapse raises concerns about the potential for mass surveillance and highlights the need for robust security measures in IoT devices. By tracking themselves, researchers demonstrated the vulnerability, emphasizing the importance of prioritizing user privacy and security.

articles.title: Lotusbail npm package found to be harvesting WhatsApp messages and contacts
articles.summary: A malicious npm package called Lotusbail has been discovered, which secretly harvests WhatsApp messages and contacts from users' devices. This package was designed to appear as a legitimate library, but instead, it exfiltrates sensitive data to a remote server. The finding highlights the growing concern of malicious packages in the npm ecosystem, posing a significant threat to user privacy and security.

articles.title: Universal Reasoning Model (53.8% pass 1 ARC1 and 16.0% ARC 2)
articles.summary: Researchers have introduced a Universal Reasoning Model that achieves impressive results on the Abstraction and Reasoning Corpus (ARC) benchmarks, passing 53.8% of ARC1 and 16.0% of ARC2 tests. This breakthrough demonstrates significant advancements in artificial intelligence's ability to reason and solve complex problems. The model's performance has sparked excitement in the AI community, with potential applications in various fields.

articles.title: Show HN: Python SDK – forecasting with foundation time-series and tabular models
articles.summary: A new Python SDK has been released, enabling developers to easily integrate foundation time-series and tabular models for forecasting into their applications. This innovation simplifies the process of predicting future trends and patterns, making it more accessible to a wider range of users. By leveraging these models, developers can build more accurate and data-driven forecasting tools, driving better decision-making and business outcomes.

articles.title: Uplane (YC F25) Is Hiring Founding Engineers (Full-Stack and AI)
articles.summary: Uplane, a startup backed by Y Combinator, is seeking founding engineers to join their team, focusing on full-stack and AI development. This opportunity allows engineers to contribute to the company's early stages and shape its technological direction. As a founding engineer, you'll play a key role in building Uplane's innovative products and services from the ground up.

articles.title: Diesel pollution particles impair lysosomal functions of iPSC-derived microglia
articles.summary: Researchers have found that exposure to diesel pollution particles can damage the lysosomal function of microglia, a type of brain cell, derived from induced pluripotent stem cells (iPSCs). This impairment can have significant implications for brain health, as lysosomes play a crucial role in removing waste and toxins from cells. The study highlights the potential neurodegenerative effects of air pollution, particularly in urban areas with high diesel emissions.
